title: BORGWARNER DECLARES QUARTERLY DIVIDEND
author: Prnewswire
description: BorgWarner Inc. (NYSE: BWA) announced a quarterly cash dividend of $0.17 per share, payable on June 15, 2026, to shareholders of record as of June 1, 2026. The declaration was made by the company's Board of Directors on April 29, 2026.

insights.sentiment_reasoning: The declaration of a quarterly dividend demonstrates the company's financial health and commitment to returning capital to shareholders. Consistent dividend payments are typically viewed positively as they indicate stable cash flows and management confidence in the company's future performance.

title: Blue Owl, Genuine Parts, And Okta Are Among Top 10 Large Cap Losers Last Week (Feb. 16-Feb. 20): Are the Others in Your Portfolio?
author: Nabaparna Bhattacharya
description: Ten large-cap stocks experienced significant declines during the week of February 16-20, 2026. Genuine Parts Company led losses with a 19.51% drop following worse-than-expected Q4 results and downward EPS guidance. Other major decliners included Blue Owl Capital (14.07%), Okta (12.86%), Flutter Entertainment (11.86%), and BridgeBio Pharma (11.43%), driven by analyst downgrades, disappointing earnings, and tender offer announcements.

insights.sentiment_reasoning: 19.51% weekly decline due to worse-than-expected Q4 financial results, FY26 EPS guidance below estimates, and multiple analyst price forecast reductions
14.07% weekly decline following announcement of tender offer by Saba Capital Management and Cox Capital Partners
12.86% weekly decline due to multiple analyst firms lowering their price targets
11.86% weekly decline; company scheduled to release Q4 and full year 2025 results, creating uncertainty
11.43% weekly decline with no specific catalyst mentioned, suggesting broader market weakness
10.59% weekly decline following UBS analyst downgrade from Neutral to Sell
6.68% weekly decline after Q4 results and downgrade by JP Morgan (Overweight to Neutral) and BMO Capital (Outperform to Market Perform) with price target cuts
4.32% weekly decline despite analyst maintaining Neutral rating, indicating broader market pressure
5.33% weekly decline attributed to CFO transition announcement (James Chuong effective March 30, 2026) rather than operational concerns

title: Rivian, Magna International, And Akamai Are Among the Top 10 Large-Cap Gainers Last Week (Feb. 9-Feb. 13): Are the Others in Your Portfolio?
author: Nabaparna Bhattacharya
description: Ten large-cap stocks were top performers during the week of February 9-13, 2026. Magna International led with a 26.81% gain following better-than-expected Q4 results and raised FY26 guidance. Other notable gainers included Generac Holdings (22.25%), Rivian Automotive (20.20%), BorgWarner (20.15%), and Akamai Technologies (17.64%), all driven by strong earnings reports and analyst upgrades.

insights.sentiment_reasoning: 26.81% weekly gain driven by better-than-expected Q4 financial results and FY26 adjusted EPS guidance above estimates
22.25% weekly gain following Q4 earnings report and analyst price target increase from $186 to $213
20.20% weekly gain after better-than-expected Q4 results and Deutsche Bank upgrade from Hold to Buy with price target raised from $16 to $23
20.15% weekly gain following better-than-expected Q4 results and announcement of master supply agreement with TurboCell
17.64% weekly gain with DA Davidson analyst maintaining Buy rating and raising price forecast from $115 to $125
20.51% weekly gain after multiple analyst firms raised their respective price forecasts
17.75% weekly gain following announcement of acquisition of Kodiak Building Partners

title: BORGWARNER DECLARES QUARTERLY DIVIDEND
author: Prnewswire
description: BorgWarner Inc. announced a quarterly cash dividend of $0.17 per share, payable on March 16, 2026, to shareholders of record as of March 2, 2026.

insights.sentiment_reasoning: The declaration of a quarterly dividend demonstrates the company's financial health and commitment to returning capital to shareholders. Regular dividend payments are typically viewed positively by investors as they indicate stable cash flows and management confidence in the company's future performance.

title: Turbocharger Market Growing at 10.62% CAGR to 2031 Says a 2026 Mordor Intelligence Automotive Report
author: Mordor Intelligence
description: The global automotive turbocharger market is projected to grow from USD 13.61 billion in 2026 to USD 22.55 billion by 2031, driven by stricter emission standards, engine downsizing, and the shift toward hybrid and hydrogen-ready powertrains. Asia-Pacific leads adoption, while electric turbochargers and variable geometry technologies are gaining traction to meet regulatory requirements like Euro 7.

insights.sentiment_reasoning: Listed as a leading turbocharger manufacturer positioned to benefit from 10.62% CAGR market growth driven by stricter emissions standards and demand for advanced turbo technologies.
Major turbocharger supplier well-positioned to capitalize on growing demand for electric-assist and variable geometry turbochargers as automakers transition to cleaner powertrains.
Through its Turbo Technologies division, positioned to benefit from growing demand for specialized turbo designs for alternative fuel vehicles and commercial vehicles.
Automotive supplier positioned to benefit from growing adoption of electric-assist and hydrogen-ready turbocharger technologies across global markets.
